On June 25, 2026, Botswana became the 68th State signatory to the Artemis Accords (the Accords), following shortly after Latvia, Jordan, Morocco, Malta, Paraguay and Ireland – the latter’s accession bringing all European Space Agency (ESA) members within the framework.
The uniformity of the text means that, in practice, the Accords function as a shared normative framework for civil space activities between the signatory States."},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/organizations/oiir/nasa-welcomes-botswana-as-68th-artemis-accords-signatory/","title":"NASA Welcomes Botswana as 68th Artemis Accords Signatory - NASA","content":"Department of State Senior Advisor for Space Gregory Autry, and Mabedi Ngwenya, acting ambassador of the Republic of Botswana to the United States, also participated in the ceremony."},{"url":"https://spacenews.com/botswana-signs-the-artemis-accords/","title":"Botswana signs the Artemis Accords - SpaceNews","content":"WASHINGTON — The African nation of Botswana is the latest country to sign the Artemis Accords outlining best practices for space exploration.
David Tshere, Botswana’s minister of communications and innovation, signed the Accords on behalf of the country during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters on June 25.
Tshere said he hoped that signing the Accords would lead to a space cooperation working group between Botswana and the United States.
“I applaud the Trump administration’s focus on securing America’s critical access to rare earth elements in Africa, and space diplomacy will play a key role in building lasting relationships,” Mike Gold, president of Redwire Space and a former NASA official who helped lead development of the Artemis Accords, told SpaceNews."},{"url":"https://africa.businessinsider.com/local/lifestyle/botswana-becomes-latest-african-nation-to-join-us-led-artemis-accords-as-africas/f0f7n58","title":"Botswana becomes latest African nation to join US-led Artemis Accords as Africa's space ambitions grow | Business Insider Africa","content":"Jun 23, 2026 — Botswana is set to become the 68th country to sign the Artemis Accords, 23 June 2026 02:26 PM. Accords in April 2026,
Accession to the Accords does not require having a space program, but signals commitment to international space cooperation.
The signing will involve Botswana’s Minister of Communications and Innovation, David Tshere, NASA Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson, and U.S.
The Artemis Accords were established in 2020 during the first Trump administration by the United States, through NASA and the Department of State, alongside seven founding nations.
The founding signatories were Australia, Canada, Italy, Japan, Luxembourg, the United Arab Emirates, the United Kingdom and the United States.
The programme has already sent the uncrewed Artemis I mission around the Moon, while later missions are expected to carry astronauts back to lunar orbit and eventually to the lunar surface.
Nigeria and Rwanda became the first African signatories in December 2022, when they joined the Accords at the inaugural U.S.-Africa Space Forum in Washington, becoming the 22nd and 23rd countries to sign globally.
Morocco then signed the Accords in April 2026, becoming the 64th country globally and the fifth African nation to join.
Unlike previous African signatories, Morocco signed the agreement in Rabat, a move that highlighted the diplomatic weight of its bilateral relationship with Washington."},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-invites-media-to-botswana-artemis-accords-signing-ceremony/","title":"NASA Invites Media to Botswana Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony - NASA","content":"The Republic of Botswana will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9:30 a.m.
Botswana will be the 68th country to sign the Artemis Accords."},{"url":"https://www.forth.news/lists/state/Cd6Sk9qsY6taurU9KUgCg","title":"United States Welcomes Serbia's Signing of the Artemis ...","date":"1 day ago","content":"1 day ago — Serbia's Minister of Foreign Affairs Marko Đurić signed the Accords on behalf of the Republic of Serbia on July 16, 2026."}]}

The ceremony would be unremarkable coalition paperwork if not for one detail the official announcement omits: just six weeks ago, Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić called his state visit to Beijing the most important of his career, walked away with China's Friendship Medal, and confirmed that the first satellite his country will ever launch is being built with technology from a subsidiary of China's national space authority. The Artemis Accords are not a treaty. Drafted by NASA and the U.S. Department of State and first signed on October 13, 2020, the Accords are a set of non-binding political commitments — they exist outside the UN Charter's Article 102 treaty framework, which means they carry no enforcement mechanism, no dispute resolution process, and no liability structure if a signatory acts inconsistently with the document's principles, as Britannica's analysis of the Accords confirms.","score":0.6737291,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/leonard.david.505/posts/on-june-25-2026-botswana-became-the-68th-nation-to-sign-the-artemis-accords-nasa/10166013644390832","title":"Botswana signs NASA's Artemis Accords","content":"On June 25, 2026, Botswana became the 68th nation to sign the Artemis Accords.
